shlokas.name: Shri Suktam — The Vedic Hymn to Lakshmi (6 Key Verses)
shlokas.purpose: The most ancient and powerful hymn to Goddess Lakshmi, from the Rigveda. Recited during Diwali Lakshmi Puja and all Lakshmi worship.
shlokas.when_to_chant: During Lakshmi Puja on Diwali, Kojagari Purnima, and Fridays. Also chanted when beginning any new financial venture.

verses.title: Opening invocation of Lakshmi
verses.sanskrit:
हिरण्यवर्णां हरिणीं सुवर्णरजतस्रजाम् ।
चन्द्रां हिरण्मयीं लक्ष्मीं जातवेदो म आवह ॥
verses.iast:
hiraṇyavarṇāṃ hariṇīṃ suvarṇarajatasrajām |
candraṃ hiraṇmayīṃ lakṣmīṃ jātavedo ma āvaha ||
verses.english_meaning: O Jataveda (the all-knowing fire), bring to me Lakshmi — she who is golden in complexion, deer-like in grace, wearing garlands of gold and silver, radiant as the moon, herself made of gold.

word_meanings.suvarṇarajatasrajā: wearing garlands of gold and silver
word_meanings.candraṃ: one who is like the moon (radiant, cool, beautiful)
word_meanings.jātaveda: fire — literally 'one who knows all births/knowledge'

verses.significance: The first verse calls Lakshmi through Agni (fire) — everything offered in fire (yajna) reaches the gods. This verse asks Agni to be the messenger, to bring Lakshmi to you.

verses.title: Lakshmi who brings abundance
verses.sanskrit:
तां म आवह जातवेदो लक्ष्मीमनपगामिनीम् ।
यस्यां हिरण्यं विन्देयं गामश्वं पुरुषानहम् ॥
verses.iast:
tāṃ ma āvaha jātavedo lakṣmīmanapagāminīm |
yasyāṃ hiraṇyaṃ vindeya gāmaśvaṃ puruṣānaham ||
verses.english_meaning: O Jataveda, bring to me that Lakshmi who does not depart once she comes. With her grace, I shall find gold, cows, horses, and good people (servants/helpers/family).
verses.significance: This verse names the specific boon: not just any wealth, but Lakshmi who STAYS. The Hindu tradition recognizes that Lakshmi is fickle (Chanchala) — she leaves the lazy, the impure, the dishonest. This prayer asks for the stable form of her grace.

verses.title: Lakshmi who is Padma — the lotus goddess
verses.sanskrit:
अश्वपूर्वां रथमध्यां हस्तिनादप्रबोधिनीम् ।
श्रियं देवीमुपह्वये श्रीर्मा देवी जुषतांम् ॥
verses.iast:
aśvapūrvāṃ rathamadhyāṃ hastinādaprabodhināim |
śriyaṃ devīmupahvaye śrīrmā devī juṣatāṃm ||
verses.english_meaning: She who is preceded by horses, who rides in the middle of a chariot, who is awakened by the sound of elephants — I call that Shri Devi here. May the Goddess Shri be pleased with me.

word_meanings.rathamadhyā: in the center of a chariot
word_meanings.hastinādapraborhinī: awakened/heralded by the sound of elephants

verses.significance: This verse describes Lakshmi arriving as royalty — the elephants (Airavata), horses, chariot. This is the famous image of Gajalakshmi (Lakshmi flanked by two elephants pouring water). Lakshmi comes where there is honor, preparation, and welcome.

verses.title: Lakshmi born from the cosmic lotus
verses.sanskrit:
कांसोस्मितां हिरण्यप्राकारामार्द्रां ज्वलन्तीं तृप्तां तर्पयन्तीम् ।
पद्मे स्थितां पद्मवर्णां तामिहोपह्वये श्रियम् ॥
verses.iast:
kāṃsosmitāṃ hiraṇyaprākārāmārdrāṃ jvalantīṃ tṛptāṃ tarpayantīm |
padme sthitāṃ padmavarṇāṃ tāmihopahvaye śriyam ||
verses.english_meaning: She who smiles like gold (bronze smile), surrounded by a golden fortress, moist/tender, blazing (with energy), content in herself, satisfying others, standing on a lotus, lotus-complexioned — I invoke that Shri here.

word_meanings.ādrā: moist, tender — the opposite of dry/harsh; compassion flows from her
word_meanings.tṛptā: content, satisfied in herself — Lakshmi is not hungry for more

verses.title: Removing poverty — the most requested blessing
verses.sanskrit:
क्षुत्पिपासामलां ज्येष्ठामलक्ष्मीं नाशयाम्यहम् ।
अभूतिमसमृद्धिं च सर्वां निर्णुद मे गृहात् ॥
verses.iast:
kṣutpipāsāmalāṃ jyeṣṭhāmalakṣmīṃ nāśayāmyaham |
abhūtimasamṛddhiṃ ca sarvāṃ nirṇuda me gṛhāt ||
verses.english_meaning: I destroy Alakshmi — the elder sister of Lakshmi, who is the impurity of hunger and thirst. Drive away all misfortune and lack of prosperity from my home.
verses.significance: This verse names Alakshmi — the opposite of Lakshmi. Alakshmi represents poverty, strife, ill-omen, the energy that drives away abundance. Traditional belief: Alakshmi leaves where there is cleanliness, the sound of mantras, the smell of incense, and the worship of Lakshmi. This is why houses are cleaned before Diwali.

verses.title: Lakshmi's abode — the lotus
verses.sanskrit:
पद्मप्रिये पद्मिनि पद्महस्ते पद्मालये पद्मदलायताक्षि ।
विश्वप्रिये विष्णुमनोऽनुकूले त्वत्पादपद्मं मयि सन्निधत्स्व ॥
verses.iast:
padmapriye padmini padmahaste padmālaye padmadalāyatākṣi |
viśvapriye viṣṇumano'nukūle tvatpādapadmaṃ mayi sannidhatsva ||
verses.english_meaning: O beloved of the lotus, O Padmini, O lotus-handed, O one who abides in the lotus, O one with eyes as wide as lotus petals, O beloved of the world, O one who is pleasing to Vishnu's heart — place your lotus feet near me.
verses.significance: Seven lotus epithets in one verse — the lotus is Lakshmi's symbol because it rises from mud without being stained by it. She represents purity in prosperity, beauty amid chaos.

shlokas.how_to_chant: Ideally recite all 15 verses of Shri Suktam in full. If not, these 6 verses capture the essential prayer. Chant while looking at the lotus flower, or while burning a ghee lamp. Best time: Friday evenings, Diwali Puja, or any new financial beginning.

shlokas.name: Mahalakshmi Ashtakam — 8 Verses to the Great Lakshmi
shlokas.purpose: Highly concentrated hymn to Mahalakshmi — each of 8 verses names a different aspect of her power
shlokas.when_to_chant: During Lakshmi Puja on Diwali, Fridays, Kojagari Purnima — or daily in front of Lakshmi's image

verses.sanskrit:
नमस्तेऽस्तु महामाये श्रीपीठे सुरपूजिते ।
शङ्खचक्रगदाहस्ते महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते ॥
verses.iast:
namaste'stu mahāmāye śrīpīṭhe surapūjite |
śaṃkhacakragadāhaste mahālakṣmi namo'stu te ||
verses.english_meaning: Salutations to You, O great Mahamaya, worshipped on the auspicious throne (Sripitha), adored by the gods. O Mahalakshmi holding the conch, discus, and mace — salutations to You.

word_meanings.mahāmāyā: the great Maya/illusion — she is the power that makes the world appear

verses.sanskrit:
नमस्ते गरुडारूढे कोलासुरभयङ्करि ।
सर्वपापहरे देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते ॥
verses.iast:
namaste garuḍārūḍhe kolāsurabhayaṃkari |
sarvapāpahare devi mahālakṣmi namo'stu te ||
verses.english_meaning: Salutations to You who ride the eagle Garuda, who is fearful to the demon Kolasura, who removes all sins — O Mahalakshmi, salutations to You.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वज्ञे सर्ववरदे सर्वदुष्टभयङ्करि ।
सर्वदुःखहरे देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते ॥
verses.iast:
sarvajñe sarvavarade sarvaduṣṭabhayaṃkari |
sarvaduḥkhahare devi mahālakṣmi namo'stu te ||
verses.english_meaning: O all-knowing, O granter of all boons, O terrible to all evil-doers, O remover of all sorrows — O Mahalakshmi, salutations to You.

verses.title: Closing verse — the complete fruit
verses.sanskrit:
महालक्ष्म्यष्टकं स्तोत्रं यः पठेद्भक्तिमान्नरः ।
सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नोति राज्यं प्राप्नोति सर्वदा ॥
verses.iast:
mahālakṣmyaṣṭakaṃ stotraṃ yaḥ paṭhedbhaktimānnараḥ |
sarvasiddhimavāpnoti rājyaṃ prāpnoti sarvadā ||
verses.english_meaning: The devoted person who reads this Mahalakshmi Ashtakam hymn — obtains all perfections and prosperity always.

shlokas.name: Mahalakshmi Gayatri
shlokas.purpose: The Gayatri mantra form of Lakshmi worship — structured like the Vedic Gayatri for meditation
shlokas.when_to_chant: During Lakshmi Puja, on Fridays, or at sunrise/sunset facing the rising/setting sun
shlokas.sanskrit:
ॐ महालक्ष्म्यै च विद्महे विष्णुपत्न्यै च धीमहि ।
तन्नो लक्ष्मीः प्रचोदयात् ॥
shlokas.iast:
oṃ mahālakṣmyai ca vidmahe viṣṇupatnyai ca dhīmahi |
tanno lakṣmīḥ pracodayāt ||
shlokas.english_meaning: We know Mahalakshmi. We meditate on the wife of Vishnu. May that Lakshmi inspire and enlighten us.
shlokas.structure_note: Gayatri form = 'We know... We meditate on... May that [deity] enlighten us.' This is the pattern of all Gayatris, originating from the famous Savitri Gayatri (Om Bhur Bhuva Svaha). Here applied to Lakshmi, it becomes a meditative practice, not just a wish.

shlokas.name: Lakshmi Puja Sankalpa
shlokas.purpose: Declaration of intent before beginning the puja — specific to prosperity and family well-being
shlokas.when_to_chant: At the beginning of Lakshmi Puja, holding flowers and water
shlokas.sanskrit:
ममात्मनः परिवारस्य च समृद्ध्यर्थं
धनधान्यसम्पदप्राप्त्यर्थं
श्रीमहालक्ष्मीप्रसादसिद्ध्यर्थं
श्रीमहालक्ष्मीपूजनं करिष्ये ।
shlokas.iast:
mamātmanaḥ parivārasya ca samṛddhyarthaṃ
dharaṇadhānyasampada prāptyarthaṃ
śrīmahālakṣmīprasādasiddhyarthaṃ
śrīmahālakṣmīpūjanaṃ kariṣye |
shlokas.english_meaning: For my own and my family's prosperity, for the attainment of wealth, grain, and abundance, and for the grace of Mahalakshmi — I perform the worship of Mahalakshmi.
